title: Nye Lauded for Cybersecurity Leadership
summary: Harvard University Distinguished Service Professor and former Harvard Kennedy School Dean Joseph S. Nye was honored by the Center’s Cyber Security Project in April for his leadership and contributions to cybersecurity through the Belfer Center’s cybersecurity initiatives. Nye has announced his retirement at the end of the semester.
body: Harvard University Distinguished Service Professor and former Harvard Kennedy School Dean Joseph S. Nye was honored by the Center’s Cyber Security Project in April for his leadership and contributions to cybersecurity through the Belfer Center’s cybersecurity initiatives. Nye has announced his retirement at the end of the semester. Michael Sulmeyer , director of the Center’s Cyber Security Project and host of the event, praised Nye for his leadership in identifying and exploring solutions to current and future cybersecurity challenges and for increasing awareness of cyber issues through his teaching and Belfer Center activities. “On this occasion,” Sulmeyer said, “we honor Joe’s dedication to stitching ‘cyber’ into the fabric of the Belfer Center and paving the way for the project we have today. Despite his upcoming retirement, we look forward to roping him into the study of cybersecurity at the Belfer Center and the Kennedy School for many years to come.” Nye began working on cyber issues with others at the Center several years ago when serious study of cybersecurity was in its infancy. He said he was reluctant at first to launch into a field that he knew little about, but he became convinced that he might be able to help raise awareness of the growing importance of cyber in national and international security. In addition to Sulmeyer, several other members of the Center community who work on cyber issues—including Ryan Ellis , David Sanger , and John Holdren —toasted Nye for his continuing contributions to the field. The event also honored Nye’s long-time assistant, Jeanne Marasca , who plans to retire this summer. The latest from Belfer See more Recommended citation "Nye Lauded for Cybersecurity Leadership." Belfer Center Newsletter, Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School (Summer 2017). _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: In April, Harvard University Distinguished Service Professor Joseph S. Nye was honored by the Belfer Center's Cyber Security Project for his leadership and contributions to cybersecurity. Project director Michael Sulmeyer praised Nye for identifying solutions to cybersecurity challenges and raising awareness through teaching and Center activities. Nye began working on cyber issues when the field was nascent, initially hesitant but convinced of its growing importance for national and international security. Other Center members, including Ryan Ellis, David Sanger, and John Holdren, also celebrated Nye's contributions. The event additionally honored Nye's long-time assistant, Jeanne Marasca, who is also retiring.
result.main_content_zh: 2025年4月，哈佛大学杰出服务教授、前哈佛肯尼迪学院院长约瑟夫·奈因在网络安全的领导力和贡献，获得了贝尔弗科学与国际事务中心网络安全项目的表彰。项目主任迈克尔·苏尔迈耶赞扬了奈在识别和探索当前及未来网络安全挑战解决方案方面的领导作用，以及他通过教学和中心活动提升网络问题意识的工作。奈在网络安全研究尚处起步阶段时便开始在该领域工作，最初虽有犹豫，但最终确信自己能够帮助提升人们对网络议题在国家和国际安全中日益增长的重要性的认识。中心其他从事网络工作的成员，如瑞安·埃利斯、大卫·桑格和约翰·霍尔德伦，也一同向奈的持续贡献致敬。此次活动还表彰了奈的长期助理珍妮·马拉斯卡，她计划于今年夏天退休。
